title: SketchEllipticalArcs Object
description: The collection of elliptical arcs in a sketch. This provides access to the existing elliptical arcs and supports the methods to create new elliptical arcs.

methods.name: addByAngle
methods.description: Creates an elliptical sketch arc where the sweep of the arc is defined by the start and sweep angles.

methods.name: addByEndPoints
methods.description: Creates an elliptical sketch arc where the sweep of the arc is defined by two points.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch elliptical arc using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of elliptical arcs in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

addByAngle.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.addByAngle Method
addByAngle.description: Creates an elliptical sketch arc where the sweep of the arc is defined by the start and sweep angles.
addByAngle.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchEllipticalArc or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: The center point of the ellipse. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: majorAxis
parameters.description: The direction of the major axis. The magnitude of this vector defines the major radius.

parameters.name: minorAxis
parameters.description: The direction of the minor axis. The magnitude of this vector defines the minor radius. This vector should be perpendicular to the major axis.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The start angle of the elliptical arc in radians, where 0 is along the major axis.

parameters.name: sweepAngle
parameters.description: The sweep angle of the elliptical arc in radians, where a positive value is counterclockwise.

addByEndPoints.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.addByEndPoints Method
addByEndPoints.description: Creates an elliptical sketch arc where the sweep of the arc is defined by two points.
addByEndPoints.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchEllipticalArc or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: The center point of the ellipse. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: majorAxis
parameters.description: The direction of the major axis. The magnitude of this vector defines the major radius.

parameters.name: minorAxis
parameters.description: The direction of the minor axis. The magnitude of this vector defines the minor radius. This vector should be perpendicular to the major axis.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the elliptical arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object. The point should lie on the defined ellipse.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the elliptical arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object. The point should lie on the defined ellipse and the elliptical arc is defined by a counterclockwise sweep from the start point to the end point.

classType.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch elliptical arc using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of elliptical arcs in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchEllipticalArcs_var.count

isValid.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchEllipticalArcs_var.isValid

objectType.title: SketchEllipticalArcs.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchEllipticalArcs_var.objectType
